hints: The limit point is the sum of the independent left-endpoint increments at each step.
Step k adds an independent U(0,(1/2)ᵏ), with variance ((1/2)ᵏ)²/12.
solution: The convergence point is $P=\sum_{k\ge1}U_k$ where $U_k\sim U(0,(1/2)^k)$ are independent, with $\mathrm{Var}(U_k)=\dfrac{(1/4)^k}{12}$. Summing, $\mathrm{Var}(P)=\dfrac1{12}\sum_{k\ge1}(1/4)^k=\dfrac1{12}\cdot\dfrac13=\dfrac1{36}.$

hints: Split the hour into two independent 30-minute halves with crash probability p each.
No crash in the hour = no crash in both halves: (1−p)²=1/9.
solution: Let $p$ be the probability of a crash in $30$ minutes. The two halves are independent, so $(1-p)^2=P(\text{no crash in hour})=\tfrac19$, giving $1-p=\tfrac13$ and $p=\tfrac23.$

hints: p_k = ½p_{k−1} + ½p_{k−2}, with p_0=1.
Largest is at k=2: 2/3 + 1/12 = 3/4.
solution: Position $k$ is reached from $k-1$ or $k-2$, so $p_k=\tfrac12p_{k-1}+\tfrac12p_{k-2}$ with $p_0=1$. The solution is $p_k=\tfrac23+\tfrac13(-\tfrac12)^k$, maximized at $k=2$: $p_2=\tfrac23+\tfrac1{12}=\tfrac34.$

hints: β = P(accept H₀ | p=0.7) = P(X not in rejection region).
Acceptance region |x−18|<4 means 15 ≤ X ≤ 21, with X~Bin(36,0.7).
solution: $\beta$ is the probability of accepting $H_0$ when $p=0.7$, i.e. $X\in\{15,\dots,21\}$ for $X\sim\mathrm{Bin}(36,0.7)$. Summing, $\beta=\sum_{k=15}^{21}\binom{36}{k}(0.7)^k(0.3)^{36-k}\approx0.0915.$

hints: Each draw-and-refill multiplies the wine fraction by (10−x)/10.
solution: Each ladle-and-refill scales the wine fraction by $\tfrac{10-x}{10}$, so after two days $\big(\tfrac{10-x}{10}\big)^2=\tfrac12$, giving $10-x=5\sqrt2$ and $x=10-5\sqrt2$. With $a=10,b=5,c=2$ (and $a=bc$), $a+b+c=17.$

hints: P(sum 7)=2[p₁p₆+p₂p₅+p₃p₄] as a function of x.
Differentiate the bracket: −1/6 + (3/10 − 4x) = 0.
solution: $P(7)=2\big[\tfrac1{36}+\tfrac{1/5-x}{6}+(\tfrac1{10}+2x)(\tfrac15-x)\big]$. Differentiating the bracket gives $-\tfrac16+(\tfrac3{10}-4x)=0$, so $\tfrac2{15}=4x$ and $x=\tfrac1{30}.$

hints: With middle age m and spacing s, Σ(m+is)² for i=−4..4 = 9m²+60s².
Need 9m²+60s² = d² with d ≤ 60.
solution: The nine ages are $m+is$, $i=-4..4$, with $\sum(m+is)^2=9m^2+60s^2=d^2$. Searching $d\le60$, $d=48$ works: $2304=9(14)^2+60(3)^2$, i.e. $m=14,s=3$ giving ages $2,5,\dots,26$. The father is $48.$

hints: All intervals share a point ⟺ every left endpoint precedes every right endpoint.
With n=5 intervals and 10 points, this forces the 5 smallest points to be the lefts: matchings = n!, total = (2n−1)!!.
solution: A common point exists iff $\max(\text{lefts})<\min(\text{rights})$, i.e. the $5$ smallest of the $10$ points are all left endpoints. Such pairings number $5!$ out of $(2\cdot5-1)!!=945$ matchings, so $P=\dfrac{120}{945}=\dfrac{8}{63}$ and $p+q=71.$

hints: First find the Δ of the K=6 call (use Γ to move from the ATM Δ≈0.5).
Plug that Δ into σ(x)=(x−0.5)²+0.3.
The implied vol rounds to 0.31.
The first sock is any color; only the second matters.
Of the 19 remaining socks, exactly 1 matches.
solution: After drawing any first sock, exactly $1$ of the remaining $19$ socks matches its color, so $P=\tfrac1{19}.$

hints: Adding all pair sums gives 2(w₁+w₂+w₃)=19+21+28=68, so the total is 34.
Each weight = 34 − (the pair sum excluding it): 15, 13, 6.
solution: Summing the pair masses, $2(w_1+w_2+w_3)=68$, so the total is $34$. Each weight is $34$ minus the opposite pair: $15,13,6$. Then $w_1^2+w_2^2+w_3^2=225+169+36=430.$

hints: Set up states by the current trailing run; let μ be expected additional flips.
Solve μ_H=1+½μ_T+½μ_HH etc.; by symmetry the post-first-flip expectation is 6.
Add the first flip: 7.
solution: With states for the current trailing run, $\mu_H=1+\tfrac12\mu_T+\tfrac12\mu_{HH}$, $\mu_{HH}=1+\tfrac12\mu_T$, and symmetric equations for tails. Solving gives $\mu_H=\mu_T=6$, so after the first flip $6$ more are expected; total $=7.$
